subject: Public Health
title: Yellow Fever and the Slave Trade
description: A fast breakdown of Yellow Fever and the Slave Trade for builders.

hook.text: Yellow fever came to the Americas on slave ships—carried by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes in water barrels.

buildup.text: The virus devastated port cities from Havana to Philadelphia, killing thousands each summer.

discovery.text: Enslaved Africans had partial immunity from childhood exposure—colonizers exploited this mercilessly.

twist.text: In Haiti, yellow fever killed 50,000 French soldiers, helping enslaved people win their revolution.

climax.text: Walter Reed proved mosquitoes transmitted it in 1900—leading to mosquito control and the Panama Canal.

punchline.text: A mosquito-borne virus changed the fate of empires.

quiz.question: How did yellow fever affect the Haitian Revolution?
options: It killed 50,000 French soldiers, helping the revolution succeed
